skill.name: conference-poster-pitch
skill.description: Generate structured elevator pitches for academic poster sessions in 30-second, 60-second, and 3-minute formats.
skill.summary: Generate elevator pitches for academic poster presentations with adjustable duration and structured output.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 44 potential issues across 4 files, driven primarily by Markdown backtick formatting in SKILL.md being misidentified as shell execution and the word 'execution' triggering cryptographic pattern matchers. After semantic evaluation of all files, every finding is a false positive: backticks are standard Markdown code spans and fenced code blocks, the Python script at scripts/main.py is a simple argparse-based pitch generator with no network, crypto, or file I/O operations, and path traversal references appear only in the security checklist documenting what to prevent. The only actual risk factor is a single documentation URL linking to the source GitHub repository. No prompt injection attempts were detected. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL linking to the source GitHub repository. This is a static documentation reference, not an active API call, and does not exfiltrate data. Poses minimal risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Formatting Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 31 instances of backtick characters in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are standard Markdown inline code spans (e.g., `scripts/main.py`) and fenced code blocks used for formatting documentation. No actual shell execution occurs in these locations.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal References in Security Checklist
low_findings.description: SKILL.md lines 125 and 128 contain '../' references in the security checklist section documenting what the skill should prevent (e.g., 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'). These are documentation of security controls, not actual path traversal attempts.

content.value_statement: Preparing a verbal pitch for a poster session is stressful without a practiced script. This skill generates structured elevator pitches in 30-second, 60-second, and 3-minute formats so you can present your research with confidence at any conference.
actual_capabilities: Generate 30-second, 60-second, and 3-minute elevator pitches for academic posters
Accept custom poster titles and duration parameters via command-line interface
Produce structured pitch scripts with clear opening, body, and closing segments
Provide ready-to-practice delivery text formatted for verbal presentation
Gracefully handle missing inputs by requesting only the minimum additional information
Return reproducible outputs through deterministic script-based execution
limitations: Requires a poster title as mandatory input before generating any pitch
Pitch content is template-based and may need manual customization for specific audiences
Does not analyze or read actual poster content or research data
Output is limited to English language only

use_cases.title: Researcher Preparing for a Conference
use_cases.description: A PhD student needs a polished elevator pitch for their poster presentation at an upcoming international conference.

use_cases.title: Lab Group Pitch Standardization
use_cases.description: A research lab wants to generate consistent pitches for multiple posters before a departmental poster session.
use_cases.target_user: Research lab managers and principal investigators

use_cases.title: Last-Minute Presentation Preparation
use_cases.description: A conference attendee needs a structured pitch template moments before the poster session begins.
use_cases.target_user: Conference presenters with limited preparation time

prompt_templates.title: Standard 60-Second Pitch
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 60-second elevator pitch for my poster titled 'CRISPR Therapy for Sickle Cell Disease'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Default pitch duration suitable for most poster session interactions

prompt_templates.title: Quick 30-Second Introduction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second pitch for my poster 'Novel Biomarkers in Cancer Detection'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Short introduction for busy conference hallways or brief encounters

prompt_templates.title: Detailed 3-Minute Presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 3-minute detailed pitch for my poster 'Machine Learning Applications in Drug Discovery'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extended discussion format for interested researchers who want more depth

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Posters Batch Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need pitches for three posters: 'AI in Radiology', 'Gene Therapy Advances', and 'Protein Folding Models'. Generate 60-second pitches for each and confirm all outputs are complete.
prompt_templates.scenario: Managing multiple poster presentations at a single conference

output_examples.input: Generate a 60-second pitch for a poster titled 'CRISPR Therapy for Sickle Cell Disease'
output_examples.output: 60s Pitch: Hi, I am presenting CRISPR Therapy for Sickle Cell Disease. Our approach targets the BCL11A gene using CRISPR-Cas9 to reactivate fetal hemoglobin production. We observed a 70 percent reduction in vaso-occlusive crises in our preliminary patient cohort. The key advantage is a one-time genetic treatment that reduces lifetime disease burden compared to ongoing transfusion therapy. I welcome questions about our delivery method and clinical trial design.

output_examples.input: Create a 30-second pitch for 'Novel Biomarkers in Cancer'
output_examples.output: 30s Pitch: Hi, I am working on Novel Biomarkers in Cancer. The key finding is that a panel of three microRNA markers achieved 94 percent sensitivity for early-stage pancreatic cancer detection. We validated this across 500 patient samples from three clinical sites. This approach could enable routine screening in high-risk populations where current methods often miss early-stage disease.

best_practices: Provide a clear, specific poster title so the pitch accurately reflects your research topic
Specify the duration to match your conference session format and time constraints
Review the generated pitch and customize names, numbers, and key findings to match your actual research
anti_patterns: Do not expect the skill to read or analyze your actual poster file or research data
Do not skip providing the poster title as it is required for pitch generation
Do not deliver the generated pitch verbatim without verifying the template content against your real research results

faq.question: What durations are supported for pitches?
faq.answer: The skill supports three durations: 30 seconds, 60 seconds, and 3 minutes.

faq.question: Do I need to install any Python packages?
faq.answer: No additional packages are required. The script uses only the Python standard library.

faq.question: Can this skill read or analyze my poster content?
faq.answer: No, the skill generates pitches from the poster title only. It cannot analyze poster files or research data.

faq.question: How do I run the pitch generator?
faq.answer: Run python scripts/main.py --poster-title "Your Title" --duration 60 in your terminal.

faq.question: What happens if I do not provide a poster title?
faq.answer: The skill stops and requests the missing title. It will not proceed until a title is provided.

faq.question: Can I modify the pitch after it is generated?
faq.answer: Yes, the generated pitch is a starting point. You should customize it to match your speaking style and actual research data.
